title: nasturtium terrestre var. semipinnatifidum
intro: Nasturtium terrestre var. semipinnatifidum, also known as the crevice nasturtium, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its unique, semi-pinnate leaves and delicate, bright yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant agent. The health benefits associated with consuming nasturtium flowers and leaves include reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function, while also providing a rich source of vitamins A and C. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues.
